channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: At least 66 killed in military plane crash in Colombia, head of armed forces says
item.description: Colombian officials say that a military cargo plane with 128 people on board, most of them soldiers, crashed shortly after taking off Monday in southwestern Colombia.
item.content:encoded:
Colombian officials say that a military cargo plane with 128 people on board, most of them soldiers, crashed shortly after taking off Monday in southwestern Colombia.

(Image credit: MiPutumayo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: UK police probe possible Iran link after Jewish charity ambulances set on fire
item.description: Police in London are investigating a suspected antisemitic hate crime attack after four vehicles belonging to a Jewish ambulance service were set on fire.
item.content:encoded:
Police in London are investigating a suspected antisemitic hate crime attack after four vehicles belonging to a Jewish ambulance service were set on fire.

(Image credit: Alberto Pezzali)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: President Trump and Iranian officials have conflicting stories about ongoing talks
item.description: President Trump is ordering the U.S. military to hold off on striking Iranian power plants, citing "productive" talks with Iran, but Iranian officials deny there's any dialogue with the U.S.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is ordering the U.S. military to hold off on striking Iranian power plants, citing "productive" talks with Iran, but Iranian officials deny there's any dialogue with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Trump delays strikes on Iran's power plants for 5 days. And, ICE deploys to airports
item.description: Trump says he will deploy ICE agents to U.S. airports to help address delays. And, the president said he would delay strikes on Iranian power plants for five days.
item.content:encoded:
Trump says he will deploy ICE agents to U.S. airports to help address delays. And, the president said he would delay strikes on Iranian power plants for five days.

(Image credit: Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump says the U.S. is in talks with Iran to end the war, which Iran denies
item.description: Trump said the U.S. will postpone any strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days, even as Israel continued hitting Tehran and Iran warned it could retaliate across the Gulf.
item.content:encoded:
Trump said the U.S. will postpone any strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days, even as Israel continued hitting Tehran and Iran warned it could retaliate across the Gulf.

(Image credit: Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Ukrainians are growing more pessimistic about the prospects for peace, survey says
item.description: As the Iran war delays peace talks for Ukraine -- and global supplies of air defense missiles -- there are signs that fewer Ukrainians are willing to endure Russia's war for as long as necessary.
item.content:encoded: As the Iran war delays peace talks for Ukraine -- and global supplies of air defense missiles -- there are signs that fewer Ukrainians are willing to endure Russia's war for as long as necessary.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Palamarenko

item.title: Italy's far-right Prime Minister's political future at stake in referendum vote
item.description: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.
item.content:encoded: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: NATO allies prepare for a Russian attack in the far North
item.description: Despite tensions, U.S. and other NATO nations conduct exercises in the far North off Norway to prepare for Russian attack.
item.content:encoded: Despite tensions, U.S. and other NATO nations conduct exercises in the far North off Norway to prepare for Russian attack.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz

item.title: Israel's war strategy involves high-level assassinations. But will it win the war?
item.description: The war between the US, Israel and Iran is intensifying. NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Yossi Melman, who reports on Israeli intelligence, about the state of the conflict as it enters its fourth week.
item.content:encoded: The war between the US, Israel and Iran is intensifying. NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Yossi Melman, who reports on Israeli intelligence, about the state of the conflict as it enters its fourth week.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: What a team of investigative reporters found out about Banksy's identity
item.description: A Reuters investigative team discovered intriguing details about Banksy's identity after seeing his art pop up in Ukraine.
item.content:encoded: A Reuters investigative team discovered intriguing details about Banksy's identity after seeing his art pop up in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma
